business.headline: Sensex plunges 800 points at open as crude oil jumps on US-Iran tensionsThe S&P BSE Sensex was down 847.62 points to 76,480.57, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 249.65 points to 23,926.50 as of 9:27 am.

Will stock market crash today after PM Modi's warning on fuel and gold buying?Markets are expected to react to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's remarks asking citizens to conserve fuel and avoid buying gold for weddings for one year amid pressure from rising global energy prices.

Why PM Modi asked Indian families not to buy goldPM Modi asked families to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The appeal linked rising oil prices and gold imports to pressure on the rupee and foreign exchange reserves.

Rs 5 crore, Rs 12 crore, Rs 40 crore? What retirement means for Indians todayRs 1 crore once sounded life-changing. Today, some Indians say even Rs 10-15 crore may not guarantee retirement comfort. I spoke to families to understand what they now consider their ideal retirement corpus and why the number keeps rising.

US proposes H-1B minimum wage hike, likely to make hiring foreign workers costlierThe US Department of Labour has proposed sharply higher prevailing wages for H-1B and related visa programmes. The draft rule has drawn both support and opposition ahead of the May 26 comment deadline.

Can motor insurers reject claims for reporting accidents late?Handcrafted ByOur Editors In the fifth story in our series Motor Mess, we spoke to experts to understand why delayed accident reporting often leads to insurance claim rejections, the mistakes policyholders should avoid and what options remain if such claims are rejected.

Two inspections, one pattern: Labour off the booksIn the sixth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, multiple factory inspections conducted in 1988 expose recurring patterns of undocumented labour, hidden wage records and statutory evasion.

Petrol and diesel prices likely to be hiked before May 15Petrol and diesel prices in India are likely to be hiked before May 15 as state-run oil marketing companies face rising financial stress amid a sharp surge in global crude oil prices, sources told India Today TV.

Bringing mothers in as co-applicants: Redefining home loan ownership in IndiaFamilies are now increasingly choosing to involve mothers in building long-term financial assets and securing a stronger financial future for themselves.

From flowers to financial freedom: Rethinking Mother's DayFor years, Mother's Day has been about flowers and celebrations. But slowly, conversations are shifting towards something deeper, i.e., helping mothers build savings, investments, and financial confidence of their own.

Explained: Why SBI shares tumbled nearly 7% after Q4 resultsSBI shares fell sharply after the lender's Q4 earnings disappointed the market. The reaction underscored investor concern over treasury income, margins and earnings quality.

Sensex closes over 500 points lower as SBI tumbles nearly 7% after Q4 resultsBenchmark indices closed sharply lower on Thursday after SBI's weaker-than-expected Q4 results hit banking shares. The decline reflected caution around crude oil prices, West Asia tensions and the broader earnings season.

Sensex sinks 600 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Benchmark indices fell sharply on Thursday as higher crude prices, foreign selling and weak banking stocks hit sentiment. Investors stayed cautious ahead of key Q4 results, with traders watching US-Iran tensions and oil moves.

I broke my FD 3 times: When it helped and when it hurtA fixed deposit feels safe because it is supposed to sit quietly in the background, growing slowly over time. But when life throws sudden surprises, that safety net often becomes the first thing people reach for. The real question is, when does breaking an FD actually make sense, and when does it backfire?

Think PAN is needed only for ITR Filing? 7 transactions where you must quote itPAN has quietly become one of the most important financial documents in India today, but are you aware that many banking, investment and cash transactions cannot be completed without quoting it?

8th Pay Commission: The family unit formula behind salary revisions of govt employeesEmployee unions have urged the 8th Pay Commission to revisit the family unit formula used to fix salaries. Any revision could raise basic pay, allowances, pensions and the broader compensation bill. But what exactly is the 'family unit' formula?

Tata Trusts board meeting postponed amid Tata Sons governance, listing discussionsTata Trusts has postponed a crucial board meeting scheduled for today to May 16, as governance disputes, Tata Sons board representation, listing discussions and leadership questions continue to draw attention across corporate India.

Lenskart shares fall over 2% amid block deal buzz, IPO lock-in expiryLenskart shares fell in Thursday's trade as block deal chatter coincided with the expiry of its IPO lock-in period. The move put the spotlight on possible supply pressure even as analysts said fundamentals remain unchanged.

Sensex opens 400 points lower, Nifty slips below 24,200 as banking stocks dragThe BSE Sensex fell 422.11 points, or 0.54%, to 77,422.41 in early trade. The NSE Nifty50 slipped 129 points to 24,197.65, falling below the 24,200 mark.

Why is Maharashtra's Rs 100 Will registration move getting so much attention?Anyone who owns assets such as a flat, land, jewellery, fixed deposits, shares or mutual funds should consider making a Will. Even small savings can become the reason for family disagreements if there is no clear-written instruction from the owner.

entertainment.headline: Martin Short breaks silence on daughter Katherine's suicide, recalls her last words Martin Short has opened up about the death of his daughter Katherine Short, recalling her final words while calling the loss a "nightmare" for the family.

Dialogues, plots, MGR mentions: Vijay's films were trailers to political rise Vijay's films repeatedly hinted at his political ambition from before Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) was even registered as a party or in the picture. His filmography over the years has been a running prelude to power.

Dua Lipa sues Samsung: The $15 million TV box lawsuit explained Singer Dua Lipa sued Samsung for allegedly using her image on television packaging without permission. She is seeking at least $15 million in damages and claimed the company continued using the photo despite legal objections.

Rakul Preet steps out of safari vehicle, video sparks row. Gir officials react A viral video of actor Rakul Preet Singh stepping out of a safari vehicle at Gir National Park raised questions over wildlife norms. However, park officials have now clarified that no rule was broken.

Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath declares Krishnavataram Part 1 tax-free in UP The Uttar Pradesh government has granted tax-free status to Krishnavataram Part 1, which was released on May 7. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath announced the decision on Sunday after a special screening.

Nick Jonas shares unseen pics of Priyanka Chopra, daughter Malti on Mother's Day Singer Nick Jonas shared unseen Instagram photos of wife, actor Priyanka Chopra, and their daughter, Malti Marie, on Mother's Day. In the caption, he called Priyanka 'wind beneath our wings'.

Karuppu trailer: Suriya and Trisha take on system in action-packed drama The trailer of Karuppu shows Suriya stepping into a long-stalled court fight alongside Trisha. It frames the film as a social drama powered by legal conflict, action set-pieces and a justice-driven peg ahead of its May 14 release.

Katrina Kaif gets handwritten card from Vicky, son Vihaan on Mother's Day Katrina Kaif shared her first Mother's Day surprise from Vicky Kaushal and baby Vihaan. The handwritten note and quiet family pictures offered a personal glimpse into the couple's new life as parents.

Viral video: Emotional Trisha holds back tears at Vijay's swearing-in ceremony During Vijay's first address at his oath-taking ceremony as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, actor Trisha Krishnan was visibly emotional, holding back tears. The moment went viral in no time.

Sunil Pal: Wasn't mentally prepared for Samay Raina's roast on Kapil Sharma's show Sunil Pal said he went to The Great Indian Kapil Show prepared for a stand-up set, but the interaction featuring Samay Raina turned into a roast instead. The exchange, with jibes from both comedians, later became a talking point on social media.

Will Vijay's Jana Nayagan release soon? Producer Venkat Narayana breaks silence Vijay's Jana Nayagan producer K Venkat Narayana addressed the film's release delay. The update came soon after the actor-politician took oath as Tamil Nadu's Chief Minister on Sunday, in Chennai.

Anurag Dobhal sorry for crash, reunites with wife but won't reconcile with family YouTuber Anurag Dobhal and wife Ritika Chauhan have returned to vlogging, apologised for recent events and said they're rebuilding their lives after his crash and family dispute. The couple said they'll focus on their son and future without reconciling with his family.

Mohanlal hints Drishyam may continue beyond part 3 with possible sequels Mohanlal said at the Drishyam 3 trailer launch that Georgekutty's story may go beyond the third film. His remarks have amplified interest in the release and left the next sequel open to audience response.

Celina Jaitly alleges her children are being brainwashed in moving Mother's Day post Celina Jaitly said in a Mother's Day note that she is being denied communication with her three children during her divorce battle with Peter Haag. The post linked her custody claims in Austria with her domestic violence case in India and her vow to continue the legal fight.

Trisha greets and hugs Vijay's mother Shoba at oath ceremony Trisha Krishnan greeted Vijay's mother, Shoba Chandrasekhar, at his swearing-in ceremony in Chennai. The exchange stood out at an event marked by Vijay's first remarks as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister and his family's emotional presence.

Anupam Kher, Sanjay Dutt to Kajol: Celebrities share Mother's Day tributes Celebrities celebrated Mother's Day by sharing sweet photos, old memories and loving messages for their mothers on social media. Anupam Kher, Sanjay Dutt, Kajol, Sunny and Bobby Deol were among the stars who commemorated the occasion.

Pawan Kalyan, Kamal Haasan, celebs congratulate Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay As Vijay took the oath as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, several celebrities congratulated him on social media. Pawan Kalyan, Kamal Haasan and R Madhavan were among the celebrities who reacted to his big political moment.

Trisha to Jana Nayagan producer: Celebrities at Vijay's swearing-in ceremony Actor-turned-politician Vijay's swearing-in ceremony as the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister saw several film personalities in attendance. These include his Leo co-star Trisha, the producer of his upcoming film Jana Nayagan and longtime collaborators from his journey in cinema.

Kriti Sanon recalls losing films to star kids before Mimi: Beyond my control Actor Kriti Sanon said roles she nearly landed eventually went to star kids during her early years in films. Her experience reflected the uncertainty, bias and unequal treatment she faced as an outsider.

general news.headline: NEET 2026 paper leaked? Guess questionnaire found similar to exam in RajasthanThe Rajasthan Special Operations Group (SOG) is probing claims that nearly 600 of the 720 questions of a NEET exam allegedly resembled material circulated among students before the test, raising fresh suspicions over whether the paper was leaked or disguised as a guess questionnaire.

Think of WFH, saving petrol, not buying gold for a year: PM Modi amid oil crisisAmid growing fears of a fuel price surge due to the ongoing West Asia conflict, Prime Minister Narendra Modi appealed to everyone to reduce petrol consumption, avoid unnecessary foreign travel, and even put gold purchases on hold for a year.

A year after Op Sindoor, Asim Munir at it again, threatens 'painful' retaliationPakistan Army chief Asim Munir used the first anniversary of the 2025 conflict with India to issue a fresh warning, saying any future action against Pakistan would trigger "widespread, dangerous and painful" consequences.

Hardik Pandya unfollows MI on Insta as fallout rumours growIPL 2026: Hardik Pandya briefly appeared to unfollow Mumbai Indians on Instagram after MI's playoff exit. The account returned within minutes, but the episode intensified speculation around his future.

In Vijay as CM, Tamil Nadu gets a young CEO - The future is hereWith a blazer replacing the traditional white-and-white, files signed on stage moments after taking oath, and a governance-first message delivered with cinematic confidence, Vijay's first day as Chief Minister broke sharply from Tamil Nadu's familiar political grammar. What unfolded in Chennai was not merely a transfer of power, but the unveiling of a new political template.

Sensex crashes 1,000 points: Why is the stock market falling today?The selloff on Dalal Street was broad-based across sectors, with banking, aviation, jewellery and consumption-linked stocks witnessing heavy pressure amid concerns over rising crude oil prices and their impact on India's economy.

Why PM Modi asked Indian families not to buy goldPM Modi asked families to avoid buying gold for weddings for a year. The appeal linked rising oil prices and gold imports to pressure on the rupee and foreign exchange reserves.

Kerala CM suspense drags into day 7 amid Congress factional war, poster fightsAn intense show of strength and poster wars are underway in Kerala, with Congress workers demanding that their preferred leaders be picked as the Chief Minister, as the party's decision drags on.

Iran war hits air travel. Calcutta was the OG aviation hub long before Dubai's riseDubai's aviation hub faces an existential threat from the war in the Middle East. But what many don't know is that before Dubai's rise, India's Calcutta (now Kolkata) was a major global aviation hub. Till the 1960s, Dum Dum Airport was served by almost every major airline to the Americas and continental Europe. Today, not a single direct flight connects to anywhere beyond Southeast Asia.

Proofs of failure: Rahul Gandhi attacks government over PM's WFH appealReacting to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's "Nation First, Duty Above Comfort" message to the nation, the senior Rahul Gandhi called him "compromised" amid mounting economic pressure, saying the public was being asked "to sacrifice due to government failures."

'Common man' Vijay swaps veshti-kurta for blazer and pants at oathVijay's attire and messaging marked a political turning point in Tamil Nadu, symbolising a generational shift and the end of an era of Dravidian political norms. With Arvind Kejriwal's "aam aadmi" image recently under strain amid controversies, Vijay appears to have filled the vacuum.

Totally unacceptable: Trump rejects defiant Iran's response to US peace proposalDonald Trump says Iran has already suffered a major defeat, but warns Washington is prepared to expand military operations if negotiations collapse. The US President says American forces have completed "probably 70 percent" of their intended targets inside Iran.

Rakul Preet steps out of safari vehicle, video sparks row. Gir officials reactA viral video of actor Rakul Preet Singh stepping out of a safari vehicle at Gir National Park raised questions over wildlife norms. However, park officials have now clarified that no rule was broken.

Rahul Gandhi's advisor is a BJP fan. His real concern? Slow investment returnsRahul Gandhi responded with a joke after his investment advisor said their professional ties had endured despite the advisor's BJP support. The exchange highlighted Dhandapani's claim that Gandhi separates professional work from political views.

Video: Turkish Airlines plane tyre catches fire during landing in KathmanduOfficials said there were 277 passengers and 11 crew members on board the aircraft at the time of the incident. Some United Nations officials were also among the passengers.

Anthropic, OpenAI execs meet Hindu and Sikh religious leaders to discuss AI ethicsAnthropic and OpenAI executives reportedly met Hindu and Sikh religious leaders in New York as part of a roundtable to discuss how to instil moral values in AI. Here is what happened.

Viral video: Emotional Trisha holds back tears at Vijay's swearing-in ceremonyDuring Vijay's first address at his oath-taking ceremony as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, actor Trisha Krishnan was visibly emotional, holding back tears. The moment went viral in no time.

Live: Netanyahu says he hopes to wean Israel off US military support in a decadeUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Qatar condemned a drone attack on a cargo vessel in its territorial waters, warning that the strike threatens maritime security and commercial shipping in the Gulf amid rising tensions linked to the Iran war. The incident has heightened fears over the safety of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global energy route. Meanwhile, Iran has reportedly sent its response to a US proposal to end the war through Pakistan-mediated diplomacy. Talks are currently focused on halting hostilities and ensuring safe passage through Hormuz. Iran also warned that any deployment of US, French or British warships in the strait would escalate the crisis and provoke an immediate response. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

UPI payment at toll booth provides lead in killing of Suvendu Adhikari's aidePolice have made no arrests more than four days after Chandranath Rath was shot dead near his Madhyamgram home. Investigators believe the killing was a planned operation, with clues pointing to outside shooters and local support.

Watch: UK Army's parachute mission aids hantavirus patient on remote islandBritish military medics parachuted onto Tristan da Cunha to support a resident with suspected hantavirus after he left the MV Hondius. The unprecedented mission underscored the island's isolation as the cruise ship outbreak continued to affect British nationals.

Dua Lipa sues Samsung: The $15 million TV box lawsuit explainedSinger Dua Lipa sued Samsung for allegedly using her image on television packaging without permission. She is seeking at least $15 million in damages and claimed the company continued using the photo despite legal objections.

Indian techie dies in California after contracting rare fungal infectionA 37-year-old Indian techie died in the US after he contracted a rare fungal infection. He is survived by his wife and five-year-old son.

Congress alleges Instagram blocked Rahul Gandhi-Vijay reels, Centre reactsCongress alleged that Instagram first blocked Rahul Gandhi and Vijay's viral oath-taking ceremony video, and later blocked Gandhi's Instagram story in which he flagged the issue.

Salon owner uses fake Instagram ID to lure, abuse Hindu girls, force them to convertA salon owner in Lucknow was arrested for allegedly using a fake Instagram profile under a Hindu name to lure Hindu girls, sexually exploit them, blackmail them with obscene photos and videos, and pressure them to convert.

Martin Short breaks silence on daughter Katherine's suicide, recalls her last wordsMartin Short has opened up about the death of his daughter Katherine Short, recalling her final words while calling the loss a "nightmare" for the family.

Dialogues, plots, MGR mentions: Vijay's films were trailers to political riseVijay's films repeatedly hinted at his political ambition from before Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) was even registered as a party or in the picture. His filmography over the years has been a running prelude to power.

sports.headline: Emotional Flick fights back tears after El Clasico win, mourns father's death An emotional Hansi Flick held back tears after El Clasico win over Real Madrid, revealing that his father had passed away on the eve of the game. Barca defeated Madrid 2-0 to clinch the LaLiga title.

IPL 2026 Play of the Day: Bhuvneshwar's six trumps his four in Raipur nail-biter IPL 2026: Bhuvneshwar Kumar's four-wicket haul had already put RCB in control against Mumbai Indians in Raipur. But it was his pressure six in the final over that ultimately decided the May 10 thriller and sealed a dramatic two-wicket win.

I don't think we deserved to win: Patidar after RCB win last-ball thriller vs MI IPL 2026: Rajat Patidar admitted Royal Challengers Bengaluru made hard work of a chase they should have controlled against Mumbai Indians in Raipur. Krunal Pandya's rescue and Bhuvneshwar Kumar's late blow sent RCB top and ended Mumbai's playoff hopes.

Krunal Pandya special knocks MI out as RCB win epic last-ball thriller Royal Challengers Bengaluru dethroned SunRisers Hyderabad from the top of the IPL points table. It did not come easy as RCB were stretched to the final ball of the match, where they eventually clinched a low-scoring thriller vs MI.

Hardik Pandya unfollows MI on Insta as fallout rumours grow IPL 2026: Hardik Pandya briefly appeared to unfollow Mumbai Indians on Instagram after MI's playoff exit. The account returned within minutes, but the episode intensified speculation around his future.

Krunal Pandya tears up remembering good old MI days with Pollard and Hardik Krunal Pandya made 73 through cramps to steer Royal Challengers Bengaluru past Mumbai Indians in a final-ball chase. The innings knocked Mumbai out of the IPL 2026 playoff race and brought back memories of his bond with Hardik Pandya and Kieron Pollard.

Controversial VAR tilts Premier League title race in Arsenal's favour Arsenal beat West Ham after VAR ruled out Callum Wilson's stoppage-time equaliser for contact on David Raya. The decision inflamed accusations of inconsistency and sharpened pressure at both ends of the table.

India U17 women eye historic World Cup dream in Asian Cup quarterfinal vs China India's U17 women face hosts China in the AFC U17 Women's Asian Cup quarterfinal on Monday. A win would send India to the 2026 FIFA U17 Women's World Cup in Morocco through qualification for the first time.

Virat Kohli milestone IPL night ends in golden duck heartbreak IPL 2026: Virat Kohli's 278th IPL appearance ended with a first-ball duck as he miscued Deepak Chahar to mid-off against Mumbai Indians. The failure made it successive ducks for Kohli, a rare slump on a night that should have marked a milestone.

Despite ban, did LSG support staff use phone in dugout during CSK clash? IPL 2026: Broadcast visuals during the Lucknow Super Giants-Chennai Super Kings match appeared to show a person using a phone near the LSG camp. With IPL rules tightly restricting phone use during live play, the viral clip has sparked questions without any official clarification.

Rishabh Pant gives bizarre reason for major blunder in defeat vs CSK at Chepauk IPL 2026: Rishabh Pant defended handing Aiden Markram the final over as Chennai Super Kings chased 204. The call exposed Lucknow's muddled death-overs planning and ended Chennai's seven-year 180-plus chase drought.

Virat Kohli pauses training in Raipur to watch Urvil Patel's six-hitting barrageRCB vs MI: Urvil Patel's breathtaking knock against Lucknow Super Giants caught the attention of everyone on Sunday. Even Virat Kohli paused his warm-up routine in Raipur and remained glued to the TV screen as Urvil smashed the joint-fastest fifty in IPL history.

Vijay, at last: Urvil Patel powers CSK's first 180-plus chase in 8 yearsIPL 2026, CSK vs LSG: Josh Inglis threatened 220. Jamie Overton pulled CSK back. Then Urvil Patel took over – and Chennai finally got the Vijay they had been waiting eight years for.

Why Hardik Pandya is missing RCB vs MI? Mumbai explain captain's absenceHardik Pandya missed Mumbai Indians' IPL 2026 match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru after failing to get medical clearance. His continued absence left Suryakumar Yadav leading MI as their playoff hopes stayed on the line.

Urvil Patel hits IPL's joint-fastest fifty, celebrates with note for papaIPL 2026: Urvil Patel produced one of the most explosive knocks in IPL history on Sunday night at Chepauk. The CSK batter smashed a 13-ball fifty against LSG before celebrating the milestone with an emotional note dedicated to his father.

I'll hopefully be there: Mitchell Starc eyes 2027 ODI World CupMitchell Starc says he wants a few more years in international cricket and has not ruled out the 2027 World Cup. After retiring from T20Is, he is trying to manage his workload carefully.

1st Test: Rizwan fifty, Azan Awais hundred not enough as Bangladesh take leadBAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Azan Awais marked his debut with a century, but Pakistan conceded a 26-run first innings lead. Mehidy Hasan Miraz's five-wicket haul kept the hosts in control as the Mirpur pitch worsened steadily.

Stefanos Tsitsipas' mother blames Paula Badosa for son's decline in tennisYulia Salnikova said the intense publicity around Stefanos Tsitsipas' relationship with Paula Badosa became a burden for him. Her remarks have renewed attention on his dip in form and the role personal life has played in it.

CSK star at CM Vijay's oath ceremony in Chennai? R Ashwin joins social media banterIPL 2026: A viral lookalike of CSK pacer Anshul Kamboj at CM Vijay's oath ceremony triggered a hilarious social media intervention from R Ashwin, just hours before the Super Kings' crucial afternoon clash against Lucknow. Kamboj has been a star for CSK with the ball and their leading wicket-taker.

Come on, you Irons: Guardiola hilariously asks West Ham United to hurt ArsenalPep Guardiola appealed to West Ham after Manchester City beat Brentford 3-0 to stay in the title hunt. The City boss imitated the West Ham hammers with his hand during the post-match press conference after the Brentford win.

Watch: New dad Suryakumar gives sweets to paparazzi after birth of baby girlSuryakumar Yadav shared sweets with reporters at Mumbai airport after the birth of his daughter. He is now set to join Mumbai Indians for a must-win match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru.

Think outside the box: RR bowlers slammed by Bond after another 200-plus leakShane Bond criticised Rajasthan Royals' bowlers after they leaked 229 in a 77-run defeat to Gujarat Titans. Bond said that the bowlers need to adapt to survive in the ultra-aggressive IPL and hasn't seen a lot of innovation from across the board.

Who is Azan Awais, first Pak batter in 17 years to hit away Test century on debut?Bangladesh vs Pakistan, 1st Test: Azan Awais scored a defiant away Test hundred on debut against Bangladesh in Dhaka. The landmark innings ended Pakistan's 17-year wait and underlined the value of strong domestic red-ball cricket.

Sooryavanshi for Test cricket? Ashwin backs crowd-favourite teenager for all formatsIPL 2026: Ravichandran Ashwin has floated the idea of fast-tracking Vaibhav Sooryavanshi into India's senior side, even in Test cricket, saying the 15-year-old possesses the kind of big-hitting ability that can pull crowds to stadiums.

RCB vs MI Preview: Bengaluru seek answers in Raipur as Kohli looks to find formIPL 2026, RCB vs MI and CSK vs LSG: The IPL playoff race heats up with a blockbuster Sunday double-header as RCB face Mumbai Indians in Raipur, while Chennai Super Kings host Lucknow Super Giants at Chepauk. With qualification spots still up for grabs, every point could prove crucial in the race to the top four.

Deepika Kumari stars as Indian recurve archery team clinches World Cup goldIndia's women's recurve team beat China in a shoot-off to win World Cup gold in Shanghai. The comeback, driven by Deepika Kumari's leadership amid coaching uncertainty, ended India's wait for this title.

Carried Dhoni's Boost cutout: When long-haired Mahi left young Samson in aweSanju Samson said sharing the CSK dressing room with MS Dhoni feels like living a childhood dream. Samson recalled as a kid he was starstruck by a long-haired Dhoni and shared how he carried the cutout of the CSK legend in his personal diary.

Champions, undelivered: Why Indian sport needs a Zomato momentHandcrafted ByOur Editors They design their own kits, fly unrecognised through airports, and joke about quitting for Instagram. India's best badminton players are asking a simple question. Why doesn't this country deliver for its non-cricket champions?

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
